overview: Sentinel is a living 3D globe that watches Earth for natural disasters — earthquakes, wildfires, volcanoes, severe storms, floods, landslides and more — and quietly surfaces what's new or worsening on its own.
features: Live multi-hazard globe fed by USGS earthquakes and NASA EONET (wildfires, volcanoes, storms, floods, landslides, drought, ice, dust)
Unified hazard model with per-kind severity weighting and per-kind layer toggles
Client-side noticing engine that surfaces only new, escalating, or worsening events
Ambient globe drift and proactive auto-fly to the most severe recent event
Tactical instrument HUD, deployable as a standalone static site
